audit_items.proof_trace: The property fails at ber_bvreplace_x (L707-711): after calling ber_memrealloc_x (L708), the return value is stored directly into dst->bv_val without a NULL check; if allocation fails, ber_memrealloc_x returns NULL, and the unconditional AC_MEMCPY(dst->bv_val, ...) at L711 becomes memcpy(NULL, ...) — a null pointer dereference causing a crash. Every other allocating function in this file (e.g., ber_dupbv_x L505-508) checks for NULL and returns early; ber_bvreplace_x omits this guard, making the gap non-intentional.
audit_items.attack_scenario: An attacker sends many large LDAP Modify requests to exhaust slapd heap memory, then a follow-up Modify or schema-check operation triggers ber_bvreplace_x with a src larger than the existing dst buffer; ber_memrealloc_x returns NULL under heap exhaustion, and the subsequent memcpy(NULL, ...) segfaults slapd — achieving remote denial of service.
